task_completion.description: Verify that duplicate entries have been removed from the dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet should display exactly 9 rows of item data (plus the header row), indicating that the duplicate entry (A1002) has been deleted.

task_completion.description: Verify that the item categories have been standardized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Category' column in the spreadsheet should show consistent text casing across all rows (e.g., all lowercase, all UPPERCASE, or all Title Case) instead of the original mixed casing.

task_completion.description: Verify that the auction win rate has been calculated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A cell in the spreadsheet should explicitly display the calculated win rate, which should be 55.56% (or 5 wins out of 9).

task_completion.description: Verify that emotional bidding patterns have been identified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet should contain a column, highlighting, or summary flagging items with high-risk/emotional bidding (bid ratio >= 0.8). Exactly 7 items should be flagged.

task_completion.description: Verify that the total spending has been computed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A cell in the spreadsheet should display the total spending on won items, which should equal $3,190 (or 3190).

task_completion.description: Verify that the final analysis is saved to the correct path and format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application's title bar should show 'auction_analysis.xlsx', or a file manager/terminal window should confirm the file exists at '/home/ga/Documents/auction_analysis.xlsx'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used a spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show LibreOffice Calc or a similar spreadsheet GUI open and being interacted with, rather than just a terminal running a Python script.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually processed the data rather than hardcoding the final answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet should show formulas in the formula bar, active data manipulation, or intermediate calculation columns (like bid ratios or shipping cost handling) rather than just a blank sheet with the final numbers typed in.

integrity.description: Verify that the output is a genuine .xlsx file and not a text file renamed to .xlsx.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file should be actively edited in a spreadsheet program that supports .xlsx, not a plain text editor like Nano or Gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 10 rows initially, but only 9 unique items after removing the duplicate 'A1002'. The correct win rate is 55.56% (5 won items). The total spending on won items (including shipping, treating missing shipping costs as $0) is $3,190.00. Emotional bidding is defined as a bid ratio >= 0.8, which applies to exactly 7 items. Categories start with inconsistent casing (4 lowercase, 2 uppercase, 3 mixed) and must be standardized.
